question: Why is the skin around your eyes more delicate than the rest of your face?
options: Because it is closer to your brain
Because it is the thinnest skin on the face with fewer oil glands and collagen
Because it gets more sun exposure than other areas
Because it produces more oil than other facial areas

question: What is the main purpose of an eye cream?
options: To remove eye makeup
To address specific concerns around the delicate eye area like dark circles puffiness and fine lines
To replace your regular moisturiser
To protect the eyes from UV rays

question: What causes dark circles under the eyes?
options: Only lack of sleep
A combination of genetics thin skin blood vessels showing through poor circulation and tiredness
Only spending too much time on screens
Only dehydration from not drinking enough water

question: What causes puffy eyes in the morning?
options: Sleeping too deeply
Fluid retention during sleep allergies lack of sleep or salty foods before bed
Applying too much eye cream
Drinking too much water before sleeping

question: What is a quick home remedy to reduce morning eye puffiness?
options: Applying hot compress to eyes
Gently placing cool cucumber slices or a cold spoon under the eyes
Rubbing the eye area vigorously
Applying lemon juice under the eyes

question: Where in your skincare routine does eye cream go?
options: Before cleanser as the very first step
After toner but before moisturiser
After sunscreen as the last step
Mixed in with your moisturiser

question: What is the correct way to apply eye cream?
options: Rub it in hard using your index finger
Gently tap it with your ring finger using light patting motions
Spread it like regular moisturiser
Apply it using a cotton pad and press firmly

question: Why should you use your ring finger to apply eye cream?
options: Because it is the cleanest finger
Because it is the weakest finger and applies the least pressure protecting delicate eye skin
Because it absorbs product the best
Because the ring finger has the most even surface

question: Can regular face moisturiser replace eye cream?
options: Yes they are exactly the same product
Eye creams are specifically formulated for the delicate eye area though some people with simple needs may use a gentle moisturiser
Regular moisturiser is always better than eye cream
Eye cream is only for people over 50

question: What ingredient in eye cream helps brighten dark circles?
options: Retinol
Salicylic acid
Vitamin C or caffeine
Heavy petroleum

question: What does caffeine do in an eye cream?
options: Wakes up your skin like coffee wakes you up in the morning
Constricts blood vessels to reduce puffiness and dark circles
Deeply moisturises the under eye area
Provides anti-aging benefits like retinol

question: What ingredient in eye cream helps with fine lines and wrinkles around the eyes?
options: Caffeine
Vitamin C
Retinol or peptides
Salicylic acid

question: Should you apply eye cream to your eyelids as well as under your eyes?
options: Yes apply it all over including on the lids
Most eye creams should only go around the orbital bone not directly on the eyelid or lash line
Eye cream is only ever applied under the eye
Eye cream should be applied directly onto the lashes

question: What are crow's feet?
options: Dark spots that appear under the eyes with age
Fine lines that fan out from the outer corners of the eyes
Puffy bags under the eyes
Dark circles that extend to the cheeks

question: What lifestyle habit is most linked to worsening dark circles?
options: Exercising regularly
Consistent lack of sleep and poor sleep quality
Drinking too much water
Using too much eye cream

question: What does hyaluronic acid in an eye cream do?
options: Brightens dark circles by reducing pigment
Provides deep hydration to plump and smooth the under eye area
Reduces puffiness by constricting blood vessels
Provides SPF protection to the eye area

question: Why do under eye bags become more noticeable with age?
options: Because the skin gets oilier with age
Because the fat pads around the eyes shift and skin loses elasticity making bags more prominent
Because older people sleep less
Because older people use more eye cream

question: What is a lip balm used for?
options: To add colour and pigment to lips
To moisturise and protect lips from dryness and chapping
To exfoliate dead skin from lips
To provide SPF protection to lips only

question: Can lips get sunburned?
options: No lips are protected by their natural colour
Yes lips can burn and lip balm with SPF is recommended to protect them
Only people with very light lips get sunburned lips
Lips only burn in extreme heat above 40 degrees

question: What is the best ingredient to look for in a lip balm for very dry chapped lips?
options: Alcohol
Fragrance and mint
Shea butter beeswax or ceramides
Salicylic acid

question: Why do lips get chapped more easily than other skin on the face?
options: Because lips are always more exposed to sunlight
Because lips have no oil glands and cannot moisturise themselves
Because lip skin is the thickest skin on the face
Because lips produce too much keratin

question: What common habit actually makes chapped lips worse?
options: Applying lip balm
Drinking water
Licking your lips which removes moisture and causes more dryness
Eating fruits with vitamin C

question: What is a lip scrub used for?
options: To colour and stain the lips
To gently exfoliate dead skin from lips for a smoother appearance
To provide long-lasting moisture to dry lips
To protect lips from wind and cold

question: How often should you use a lip scrub?
options: Every single day for best results
Once or twice a week is enough as over-exfoliating can irritate lips
Only once a month
Three times a day for maximum smoothness

question: What simple DIY ingredient can be used to make a homemade lip scrub?
options: Baking soda
Sugar mixed with a little honey or oil
Flour and water
Lemon juice and salt

question: What does peptide do in an eye cream?
options: Reduces puffiness by draining fluid
Helps stimulate collagen production to firm and smooth the skin around the eyes
Brightens dark circles instantly
Provides SPF protection to the eye area

question: Can allergies cause puffy dark under eyes?
options: No allergies only affect the nose
Yes allergies can cause inflammation swelling and dark circles around the eyes
Only food allergies affect the eye area
Only seasonal allergies cause eye puffiness

question: What is an eye mask or eye patch used for?
options: To protect eyes from light while sleeping
To deliver a concentrated dose of hydrating or brightening ingredients to the under eye area
To remove eye makeup
To treat puffy lids rather than under eyes

question: What does collagen do for the skin around the eyes?
options: It reduces dark pigmentation
It provides structure and firmness to skin keeping it from sagging and wrinkling
It brightens and evens skin tone
It provides hydration similar to hyaluronic acid

question: What is the orbital bone and why does it matter when applying eye cream?
options: It is the coloured part of your eye
It is the bony ridge around your eye socket and eye cream should be applied along this ridge only
It is the inner corner of the eye where eye cream is most effective
It is a term for the under eye hollow area

question: What ingredient should you be careful using around the eyes because it can cause irritation?
options: Hyaluronic acid
Ceramides
Retinol which must be used carefully in low concentrations around the delicate eye area
Peptides

question: What causes lips to look thinner and lose volume with age?
options: Lips gain volume with age
The body produces less collagen and hyaluronic acid causing lips to thin and lose definition
Lips thin because of sun exposure only
Lips thin only if you wear too much lipstick

question: What is the best time to apply a nourishing lip treatment?
options: Only in the morning before lipstick
Before bed so lips can absorb moisture overnight
Only right after eating
Only when lips are already chapped badly

question: Why should you avoid lip balms with certain ingredients like camphor or menthol if your lips are very dry?
options: Because they permanently stain lips
Because these ingredients can feel cooling but actually cause more dryness and irritation over time
Because menthol is only for cold weather use
Because camphor removes the natural colour from lips

question: What vitamin is beneficial in both eye cream and lip care products for brightening?
options: Vitamin D
Vitamin B12
Vitamin C
Vitamin K

question: What role does vitamin K play in eye cream?
options: It brightens skin by reducing melanin
It helps reduce the appearance of dark circles by improving blood circulation
It firms the eye area by boosting collagen
It deeply hydrates dry under eye skin

question: What is a common sign that your eye cream is working well for you?
options: The eye area feels tight and dry
The area around your eyes looks more hydrated smoother and dark circles appear lighter over time
Your eye cream causes slight redness
Your eye area becomes oilier

question: Why should you store lip balm away from heat and direct sunlight?
options: Because heat makes lip balm smell different
Because heat can melt or break down the formula reducing its effectiveness and shelf life
Because cold lip balm is always more effective
Heat has no effect on lip balm

question: What is a tinted lip balm?

A lip balm that combines moisturising benefits with a hint of colour
A lip balm that contains retinol for anti-aging

question: What does niacinamide do in an eye cream?
options: Reduces puffiness by constricting blood vessels
Helps brighten dark circles and improve the overall tone and texture of the under eye skin
Provides intense hydration like hyaluronic acid

question: What is the main cause of vertical lines appearing on the upper lip area with age?
options: Too much lip balm use
Loss of collagen repetitive facial movements and smoking which is a major contributor
Eating too many acidic foods
Applying lipstick every day

question: Can sleeping position affect under eye puffiness?
options: No sleeping position has no effect on the eye area
Yes sleeping on your side or stomach can cause fluid to pool under the eyes while sleeping on your back reduces puffiness
Only sleeping without a pillow affects puffiness
Only sleeping position affects dark circles not puffiness

question: What does an SPF lip balm protect against?
options: Only extreme cold weather
UV rays that can cause sunburn dryness and long term damage to the delicate lip skin
Only wind and dry air
Only bacteria that cause cold sores

question: What is a good quick fix for very dry and flaky lips before applying lipstick?
options: Apply more lipstick on top to cover the flakes
Gently exfoliate with a soft toothbrush or lip scrub then apply a hydrating lip balm
Use a lip liner all over the lips
Apply foundation over the lips before lipstick

question: What causes the skin under the eyes to look hollow or sunken creating a dark shadow?
options: Drinking too much water
Volume loss in the under eye area with age causing a tear trough hollow that creates shadows

question: Which of these is a lifestyle change that can genuinely help improve dark circles over time?
options: Applying more concealer daily
Getting consistent quality sleep staying hydrated reducing salt and managing allergies

Applying ice directly to the under eyes every single day

question: What is beeswax commonly used for in lip balms?
options: To add a minty flavour to lip balm
To create a protective barrier that locks in moisture and protects lips from environmental damage
To brighten lip colour naturally

question: What does retinol do when used in a specially formulated eye cream?
options: Brightens dark circles by reducing melanin
Reduces puffiness by draining excess fluid
Helps stimulate cell turnover and collagen to reduce fine lines and crow's feet
Provides SPF protection to the thin eye skin

question: What is a cold eye mask and when is it most useful?
options: A mask worn at night to block out light for better sleep
A gel mask stored in the fridge that reduces puffiness and soothes tired eyes when applied cold
A mask that contains retinol for anti-aging
A mask used to treat dark circles permanently

question: What should you do if eye cream gets directly into your eye?
options: Leave it and it will dissolve naturally
Rinse your eye gently with clean water and stop using that product near the eye if irritation continues
Apply more eye cream immediately to balance it out

question: Why is the lip area one of the first places to show signs of dehydration on the face?
options: Because lips absorb the most product
Because lips have no oil glands and rely entirely on external moisture making them very vulnerable to dehydration
